what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that records an in-progress reasoning pass: it reports that one design agent is still verifying whether channels keep member registries, and it surfaces two emergent design threads for the user to choose where to go deeper next.
observations: A fifth agent is still walking the code to check whether channels maintain member registries.
The author expects the next pass to answer that registry question, which determines how member-based access and feeding are wired.
The note explicitly commits to continuing every thread and avoiding summarization.
The mark system already distinguishes positive signal from subtractive noise.
Two dragnet engines currently exist: one for reading code and one for reading transcripts/design.
The author proposes collapsing those two engines into a single engine with selectable lenses.
The note ends by asking the user which thread to deepen.
standouts: The two dragnet engines should probably become one engine with chooseable lenses.
Observations can be positive or subtractive, enabling down-weighting of wrong understandings.
An unresolved assumption about channels keeping member registries is still being verified.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI links for related items.
Markdown body uses a top-level heading and bullet lists.
Written as a first-person assistant update.
concerns: Presents speculative design directions as likely moves without showing supporting evidence in this file.
Relies on an unverified assumption about channel member registries.
Does not link to or define the mark system, the two dragnet engines, the scope ladder, or the unified web, leaving terms ambiguous from this file alone.
notes: A development checkpoint note that flags an open channels/membership assumption and offers two new design threads for deeper exploration.
questions: What is the 'fifth agent' and how does it relate to the other agents?
Where is the mark system defined and how does it distinguish signal from noise?
What are the two dragnet engines and how do they differ in implementation?
What is the 'scope ladder' and the 'unbuilt unified web'?
How would a lens be selected in the proposed unified dragnet engine and what would its interface be?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and content frame it as an in-flight status note that also proposes where to go deeper.
apparent_themes: Signal versus noise in observational systems.
Keeping threads open rather than closing them prematurely.
intention_signals: Keep multiple design threads alive, surface emergent structural choices, and invite the user to pick the next focus for deeper work.
novelty: Frames design work as parallel agents walking the code and explicitly refuses to summarize, treating the note as an open continuation rather than a conclusion.
contribution: Captures an open verification about channel member registries and two candidate design moves (subtractive observations and a lensable dragnet engine) so a fused system can preserve the unresolved threads and the reasoning behind them.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard block titled 'Still in flight, and where to go deeper' reports that an agent is still verifying whether channels keep member registries and offers two emergent design threads: observations can be subtractive as well as positive, and the separate code/design dragnet engines could be unified into one lens-selectable engine.
intention_for_embedding: To record an in-progress reasoning pass, flag an unresolved architectural assumption, and invite the user to choose the next thread to deepen.
